title: Cirrus Starter Kit Comparison Cheat Sheet

sections.content:
| Criteria       | Competitor A | Competitor B | Cirrus Starter Kit  |
|-----------------------|------------------------|------------------------|---------------------|
| Energy Efficiency (%) | 97           | 95           | 99         |
| Setup Costs ($) | 4,500         | 6,000         | 3,800        |
| Price per PB ($)  | 3.00          | 2.50          | 3.50        |
| Upkeep Costs (%)   | 0.5          | 0.8          | 0.3         |
| Return on Investment (%) | 12         | 10           | 15         |
| RTO (hrs)    | 6          | 4           | 2         |
| P1 Support SLA (min)| 60          | 30           | 15         |

Notes:
1. Energy Efficiency (%): The percentage of energy dedicated.
2. Setup Costs ($): The estimated cost of installing a starter kit system per use case [actual price will vary by use case].
3. Price per MWh ($):The cost of energy.
4. Upkeep Costs (%): Annual maintenance costs as a percentage of the setup cost.
5. Return on Investment (%): The percentage return on the initial investment over 5 years of using the service.
6. PTO (hrs): Worst-case Recovery Time Objective for recovering energy in a event of a failure.
7. P1 Support SLA (min): The support SLA for P1 support cases

Please replace the values in this table with accurate and up-to-date information based on your research or specific product details. Additionally, you may want to include additional criteria based on your project's requirements.
